human.title: Human language
human.description: What human language is this in?

license.title: What license does this have?
license.description: Note: without a license others cannot re-use the work without explicit permission

creators.title: creators
creators.description: Please separate the names by ';' so they acn be correctly processed.

DOI.title: DOI
DOI.description: If this contribution has a DOI make sure to include it here!

presenters.title: Presenters
presenters.description: Does this contribution come from a course? Is the file a video or audio medium? Then there are presenters! Please separate their names using ';'.

institutes.title: Institute(s)
institutes.description: Which research institutes were involved in the creation of this content? Please separate them using ';'.

website.title: website
website.description: Is there a website for the content? E.g. The link to a web-book, a website of the creator(s), or a page with more information.

difficulty.title: Difficulty
difficulty.description: Out of 10 how difficult of a topic is it? (10 being very challenging and 1 being easy peasy)

fundamental.title: Fundamental
fundamental.description: Is this a vital part of programming or more domain dependent?

learning_ease.title: Ease of learning
learning_ease.description: Out of 10, how easy is the material to follow and understand on your own? (10 being easy peasy and 1 being very challenging)

date_of_creation.title: Date of creation
date_of_creation.description: An approximate date will work if you do not know the exact date the content was made.

date_of_review.title: Date of review/update
date_of_review.description: Has this content been reviewed and updated at any point?

date_of_presentation.title: Date of presentation
date_of_presentation.description: Was or will this content be presented? When was that/will that be?

file_types.title: File types
file_types.description: What types of files are in your contribution? Some users might prefer audio or perhaps want to avoid larger files like videos.

pilot.title: Pilot
pilot.description: Is this content part of a pilot or has it been tried, tested, and refined?

work_in_progress.title: Work in progress
work_in_progress.description: Is this still being build upon or is this the final version? If it is a work in progress consider including the website so that people can keep up to date.

accreditation.title: Accreditation
accreditation.description: Is there any accreditation available for following or completing this?

number_of_modules.title: Number of modules
number_of_modules.description: How many modules are in this content? If it is a single item you can type '1'.

material_type.title: Material type
material_type.description: Is the material intended to be taught, self taught, or a combination?

time_to_follow.title: Time to follow
time_to_follow.description: To the nearest hour, approximately how long does it take to follow the material?

software.title: Software Requirements
software.description: What software would someone need to follow the material? Separate names with ';'.

libraries.title: Package/Library Requirements
libraries.description: What packages/libraries would someone need to install in order to follow the material? Separate names with ';'.

dependencies.title: 3rd Party Requirements
dependencies.description: Are there any 3rd party dependencies needed to follow the material? Separate names with ';'.

proprietary.title: Proprietary tools
proprietary.description: Are there any paid or closed tools or programs needed to follow the material?

account_needed.title: Account requirements
account_needed.description: Will the student need to make an account for anything to follow the material?

currency.title: Currency of cost
currency.description: Please use the 3 letter ISO 4217 code (check here for yours, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217). E.g. EUR for euros, ZMW for Zambian Kwacha, or VND for Vietnamese Dong

cost.title: Cost to follow
cost.description: What are the total costs for any paid services, tools, books, objects, data, or software needed to follow the material?

size_in_mb.title: Size of the materials in MB

mobile_friendly.title: Mobile friendly
mobile_friendly.description: Could someone follow this on a mobile, tablet, of chromebook?

sight_impaired_friendly.title: Sight impaire friendliness
sight_impaired_friendly.description: Is the material in a format that someone could use a screen reader to read the text aloud?

readability_styling.title: Readability
readability_styling.description: Does the material make use of a style guide to make it more readable (e.g. the Landelijke Docs one) I.E. does it have fonts, spacings, formatting, and colours that are friendly for those with ADHD, Dyslexis, Colour Blindness, or any other reading difficulty?
